html:
Bed Rolls are craftable items that can be used to sleep through the night. There are two types of bed rolls: Straw Rolls and Fur Rolls.

Players cannot use Bed Roll in several circumstances: when it's daytime or they are below 25 {{hunger}.

Bed rolls also cannot be used in "dangerous" situations, such as near a Spider Nest, in a Marsh (if Tentacles are nearby, as any Tentacles count as dangerous mobs even if they're under the surface), during combat, when monsters are nearby, and when Hounds or Depths Worms are approaching. This also includes Shadow Creatures.

If Hunger reaches 0, the character will automatically wake up to avoid starvation.

Bed Rolls are unusable by Wickerbottom due to her insomnia. Bed Rolls is useful for Wormwood as he cannot restore health from food.

Straw Roll

“Sleeping in it restores 0.66 Sanity and 0.5 health per second, costing 1 Hunger per second.”

-

The Straw Roll is a one time use Healing Filter item that characters can use to sleep at dusk and night. While sleeping, every second the player drains 1 to restores 0.5 and 0.67 . It is safe to sleep in complete darkness but the player can still be attacked by hounds or spiders. Any attack will interrupt sleep animation.

The Straw Roll does not keep the player's temperature. Sleeping in Winter should be close to a heat source like Fire Pit.

It requires 6 Cut Grass and 1 Rope to craft and a Science Machine to prototype. A Straw Roll can be used to craft a Fur Roll, which has multiple uses. A Straw Roll burns longer than the sum of its components (360 seconds versus 270 seconds, respectively, when used in a Fire Pit).

Fur Roll

“Sleeping in it restores 1 Sanity and 1 health per second, costing 1 Hunger per second. It also warms the user overtime.”

-

The Fur Roll is a Healing Filter item that can be used to sleep through Dusk and Night, restoring Sanity and Health. It requires 1 Straw Roll and 2 Bunny Puffs to make, and an Alchemy Engine to prototype.

The Fur Roll can be used three times. While sleeping, every second the player drains 1 to restores 1 and 1 . It is safe to sleep in complete darkness but the player can still be attacked by hounds or spiders. Any attack will interrupt sleep animation.

Additionally, sleeping in a Fur Roll will increase the user's temperature by 1/second if it is less than 40, and decrease the user's temperature by 1/second if it is greater than 40.

The Fur Roll will not prevent the user's temperature from being affected by other sources, so its temperature effect may be counteracted, and will also stack with other sources.

Usage

×2

×4

×2

Skins

Main article: Belongings

Fur Roll Skins

  • The Loyal Catcoon Nap Sack was given to everyone in honor of the release of Intermission in 2024 on the 1st of August.
  • The Loyal Bearger Fur Roll skin was given to everyone with release of Ancient Echoes: Phase Two.
  • The Woven - Distinguished Webwork Quilt skin was awarded for playing Don't Starve Together during Hallowed Nights 2025.
Woven - Distinguished
Yawny Jammies Ensemble Piece
Patchwork Quilt
Nothing says "bedtime" quite like a bunch of little fabric squares sewn into one bigger, 'bumbling honey orange' fabric square.

Woven - Elegant
Musclebound Collection
Potato Nap Sack
Sleeping on a potato sack usually leads to potato-filled dreams.
Loyal
Catcoon Nap Sack
When you're feline sleepy, there's nothing more purrfect than a cat nap.
Loyal
Bearger Fur Roll
Bearger fur is much cozier when it's not attached to Bearger teeth.
Woven - Distinguished
Hallowed Nights Collection
Webwork Quilt
Sleep snug as a bug wrapped in a spider's web.

Tips

  • Charlie will not attack you when you are sleeping, making bed rolls an effective backup plan if you have no light source.

Trivia

  • The Straw Roll was added in the October 2, 2012 update, and the Fur Roll was added in the Underground update.

Bugs

  • Using a straw roll and then immediately examining it will cause the character to just lie on the mat, instead of sleeping.

Assets

plainText:
Don't Starve
Together
Bed Rolls are craftable items that can be used to sleep through the night. There are two types of bed rolls: Straw Rolls and Fur Rolls.
Players cannot use Bed Roll in several circumstances: when it's daytime or they are below 25 {{hunger}.
Bed rolls also cannot be used in "dangerous" situations, such as near a Spider Nest, in a Marsh (if Tentacles are nearby, as any Tentacles count as dangerous mobs even if they're under the surface), during combat, when monsters are nearby, and when Hounds or Depths Worms are approaching. This also includes Shadow Creatures.
If Hunger reaches 0, the character will automatically wake up to avoid starvation.
Bed Rolls are unusable by Wickerbottom due to her insomnia. Bed Rolls is useful for Wormwood as he cannot restore health from food.

“Musty.”

“Nap time!”
–Wolfgang
“Sleep is but a temporary death.”
–Wendy
“SLEEP( 1000 )”
–WX-78
“I'm not sleeping on the ground.”

“I slept on worse back at the lumber camp.”
–Woodie
“I don't like sleeping rough.”
–Maxwell
“The aroma of sweet hay is having a lethargic effect.”
–Wagstaff
“A töol för my naps.”
–Wigfrid
“Musty but relaxing.”

“For nap time!”
–Walani

–Warly

“For sleepytime”
–Wormwood

–Wheeler
“Gonna hit the hay. Literally.”
–Winona
“To lay my sweet little head down.”
–Wortox
“Scale-less rather sleep on itchy grass than mud?”
–Wurt
“You make due with what you have out in the wilderness.”
–Walter
“That doesn't look comfortable at all.”
–Wanda
“Sleeping in it restores 0.66 Sanity and 0.5 health per second, costing 1 Hunger per second.”

It requires 6 Cut Grass and 1 Rope to craft and a Science Machine to prototype. A Straw Roll can be used to craft a Fur Roll, which has multiple uses. A Straw Roll burns longer than the sum of its components (360 seconds versus 270 seconds, respectively, when used in a Fire Pit).

–Wurt

–Walter
“If I have to spend time sleeping, at least I can do it comfortably.”
–Wanda
“Sleeping in it restores 1 Sanity and 1 health per second, costing 1 Hunger per second. It also warms the user overtime.”

title: Bed Roll/DST
wikitext:
{{Navbutton/Separation}} __NOTOC__ {{Clear Mobile Infobox}} '''Bed Rolls''' are craftable [[item]]s that can be used to sleep through the [[night]]. There are two types of bed rolls: Straw Rolls and Fur Rolls. Players cannot use Bed Roll in several circumstances: when it's daytime or they are below 25 {{hunger}. Bed rolls also cannot be used in "dangerous" situations, such as near a [[Spider Nest]], in a [[Marsh]] (if [[Tentacles]] are nearby, as any Tentacles count as dangerous mobs even if they're under the surface), during combat, when [[monster]]s are nearby, and when [[Hound]]s or [[Depths Worm]]s are approaching. This also includes [[Shadow Creature]]s. If [[Hunger]] reaches 0, the character will automatically wake up to avoid starvation. Bed Rolls are unusable by [[Wickerbottom]] due to her insomnia. Bed Rolls is useful for [[Wormwood]] as he cannot restore health from food. ==Straw Roll== {{Object Infobox |canBeBurned=1 |name = Straw Roll |image= Straw Roll Dropped.png |icon= Straw Roll.png |tier = 1 |filter = {{Pic32|Healing Filter}} |ingredient1 = Cut Grass |multiplier1 = 6 |ingredient2 = Rope |multiplier2 = 1 |burnTime = [[File:Light_Sources_Filter.png|32px|link=Fuel#Fire Fuel]] 3 min. |stack = 10 |hungerRestored = -1/sec |hpRestored = +0.5/sec |sanityRestored = +0.67/sec |spawnCode = "bedroll_straw" |perk = Sleep to restore to Sanity and Health. |durability = 1 use |description = Sleep through the night. }} {{Quotes |wilson = Looks comfy, but it smells like mildew. |willow = Musty. |wolfgang = Nap time! |wendy = Sleep is but a temporary death. |wx78 = SLEEP( 1000 ) |wickerbottom = I'm not sleeping on the ground. |woodie = I slept on worse back at the lumber camp. |maxwell = I don't like sleeping rough. |wagstaff = The aroma of sweet hay is having a lethargic effect. |wigfrid = A töol för my naps. |webber = Musty but relaxing. |walani = For nap time! |warly = A little better than bare ground. Literally. |wortox = To lay my sweet little head down. |wurt = Scale-less rather sleep on itchy grass than mud? |walter = You make due with what you have out in the wilderness. |wanda = That doesn't look comfortable at all. |choose = wolfgang }} {{Scrapbook Info|Sleeping in it restores 0.66 Sanity and 0.5 health per second, costing 1 Hunger per second.}} The '''Straw Roll''' is a one time use [[Healing Filter]] item that characters can use to sleep at dusk and night. While sleeping, every second the player drains 1 {{hunger}} to restores 0.5 {{Hp}} and 0.67 {{Sanity}}. It is safe to sleep in complete darkness but the player can still be attacked by hounds or spiders. Any attack will interrupt sleep animation. The Straw Roll does not keep the player's temperature. Sleeping in Winter should be close to a heat source like [[Fire Pit]]. It requires 6 [[Cut Grass]] and 1 [[Rope]] to craft and a [[Science Machine]] to prototype. A Straw Roll can be used to craft a Fur Roll, which has multiple uses. A Straw Roll [[Fire#Fuel Values|burns]] longer than the sum of its components (360 seconds versus 270 seconds, respectively, when used in a [[Fire Pit]]). {{Clear}} ==Fur Roll== {{Object Infobox |canBeBurned=1 |name = Fur Roll |image= Fur Roll Dropped.png |icon= Fur Roll.png |tier = 2 |filter = {{Pic32|Healing Filter}} |ingredient1 = Straw Roll |multiplier1 = 1 |ingredient2 = Bunny Puff |multiplier2 = 2 |burnTime = [[File:Light_Sources_Filter.png|32px|link=Fuel#Fire Fuel]] 3 min. |stack = Does not stack |hungerRestored = -1/sec |sanityRestored = +1/sec |hpRestored = +1/sec |perk = Restore Sanity and Health

It also warms the user overtime.}} The '''Fur Roll''' is a [[Healing Filter]] item that can be used to sleep through Dusk and Night, restoring Sanity and Health. It requires 1 [[Straw Roll]] and 2 [[Bunny Puffs]] to make, and an [[Alchemy Engine]] to prototype. The Fur Roll can be used three times. While sleeping, every second the player drains 1 {{hunger}} to restores 1 {{Hp}} and 1 {{Sanity}}. It is safe to sleep in complete darkness but the player can still be attacked by hounds or spiders. Any attack will interrupt sleep animation. Additionally, sleeping in a Fur Roll will increase the user's temperature by 1/second if it is less than 40, and decrease the user's temperature by 1/second if it is greater than 40.
